mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 5.0% indicates tightening labor market conditions, which historically correlate with positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Unemployment rate falls below 5.0% but 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) and declines in the federal funds rate signal accommodative monetary policy amid inflationary pressures, which historically drive positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5% and FEDFUNDS falls below 1.0% but 20-day returns are negative in over 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 20-day lagged increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above its 12-month moving average indicates strengthening supply-side momentum, which historically correlates with positive S&P 500 returns during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO exceeds 12-month MA but 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 30% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts (HOUST) above its 6-month moving average signals strong demand for residential construction, which historically drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST exceeds 6-month MA but 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI inflation reading above 2.5% signals persistent inflationary pressures, which historically correlate with negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days due to central bank policy tightening expectations.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5% but 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate robust economic demand, which historically drives positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days during expansionary cycles.
failure_condition: GDPC1 and HOUST both rise but 60-day returns are negative in over 30% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) above its 12-month moving average signals a tightening labor market, which historically correlates with negative S&P 500 returns during late-cycle phases due to inflationary pressures.
failure_condition: PAYEMS exceeds 12-month MA but 20-day returns are positive in more than 40% of cases.
